items.headline: Top Stock Picks for Week of July 13, 2026

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month, underperforming the Zacks Chemical - Diversified industry and the S&P 500's declines of 6.7% and 0.1%, respectively. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately.

items.headline: UBS Adjusts Price Target on Albemarle to $205 From $264
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $211.44, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Shares of Albemarle (NYSE: ALB) are flat so far this year, thanks to an oversupply of lithium and a flattened demand for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States. However, the long-term need for this critical metal is projected to increase 353% by the end of the decade.

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: RBC stated it now expects 2026 and 2027 EBITDA of $2.8 billion and $2.9 billion, respectively. The brokerage said it reiterated its outperform rating on the stock and boosted its price target to $257 per share from $253.

items.headline: Albemarle Shares Fall After Baird Downgrade
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) shares were down 6.4% in Friday afternoon trading after Baird downgraded the company's stock to neutral from outperform, with a price target of $210.
